heading: Indemnification; NASA launch services and reentry services
label: 20148 Indemnification; NASA launch services and reentry services

children.heading: Liability Insurance of the Provider.—
children.label: c Liability Insurance of the Provider.—
A
children.text: a third party for death, bodily injury, or property damage or loss resulting from a launch service or reentry service carried out under the contract; and

children.label: B
children.text: the United States Government for damage or loss to Government property resulting from a launch service or reentry service carried out under the contract.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: The Administrator shall determine the maximum probable losses under subparagraphs (A) and (B) of paragraph (1) not later than 90 days after the date that the provider requests such a determination and submits all information the Administrator requires.

children.heading: .—Revisions
children.label: B .—Revisions
children.text: The Administrator may revise a determination under subparagraph (A) of this paragraph if the Administrator determines the revision is warranted based on new information.

children.label: i
children.text: $500,000,000 under paragraph (1)(A); or

children.label: ii
children.text: $100,000,000 under paragraph (1)(B); or

children.label: B
children.text: the maximum liability insurance available on the world market at reasonable cost.

children.label: A
children.text: The Government.

children.label: B
children.text: Personnel of the Government.

children.label: C
children.text: Related entities of the Government.

children.label: D
children.text: Related entities of the provider.

children.label: E
children.text: Government astronauts.

children.heading: .—No Indemnification Without Cross-waiver
children.label: d .—No Indemnification Without Cross-waiver
children.text: Notwithstanding subsection (a), the Administrator may not indemnify a provider under this section unless there is a cross-waiver between the Administration and the provider as described in subsection (e).

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: The Administrator, on behalf of the United States and its departments, agencies, and instrumentalities, shall reciprocally waive claims with a provider under which each party to the waiver agrees to be responsible, and agrees to ensure that its related entities are responsible, for damage or loss to its property, or for losses resulting from any injury or death sustained by its employees or agents, as a result of activities arising out of the performance of the contract.

children.heading: .—Limitation
children.label: 2 .—Limitation
children.text: The waiver made by the Government under paragraph (1) shall apply only to the extent that the claims are more than the amount of insurance or demonstration of financial responsibility required under subsection (c)(1)(B).

children.heading: .—Willful Misconduct
children.label: f .—Willful Misconduct
children.text: Indemnification under subsection (a) may exclude claims resulting from the willful misconduct of the provider or its related entities.

children.heading: .—Certification of Just and Reasonable Amount
children.label: g .—Certification of Just and Reasonable Amount
children.text: No payment may be made under subsection (a) unless the Administrator or the Administrator’s designee certifies that the amount is just and reasonable.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: Upon the approval by the Administrator, payments under subsection (a) may be made from funds appropriated for such payments.

children.heading: .—Limitation
children.label: 2 .—Limitation
children.text: The Administrator shall not approve payments under paragraph (1), except to the extent provided in an appropriation law or to the extent additional legislative authority is enacted providing for such payments.

children.heading: .—Additional appropriations
children.label: 3 .—Additional appropriations
children.text: If the Administrator requests additional appropriations to make payments under this subsection, then the request for those appropriations shall be made in accordance with the procedures established under section 50915.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: The authority to indemnify under this section shall not create any rights in third persons that would not otherwise exist by law.

children.heading: .—Other authority
children.label: 2 .—Other authority
children.text: Nothing in this section may be construed as prohibiting the Administrator from indemnifying a provider or any other NASA contractor under other law, including under ( et seq.).Public Law 85–80450 U.S.C. 1431

children.label: A
children.text: all obligations under this section are subject to the availability of funds; and

children.label: B
children.text: nothing in this section may be construed to require obligation or payment of funds in violation of sections 1341, 1342, 1349 through 1351, and 1511 through 1519 of title 31, United States Code (commonly referred to as the “Anti-Deficiency Act”).

children.heading: .—Relationship to Other Laws
children.label: j .—Relationship to Other Laws
children.text: The Administrator may not provide indemnification under this section for an activity that requires a license or permit under chapter 509.

children.heading: .—Government astronaut
children.label: 1 .—Government astronaut
children.text: The term “government astronaut” has the meaning given the term in section 50902.

children.heading: .—Launch services
children.label: 2 .—Launch services
children.text: The term “launch services” has the meaning given the term in section 50902.

children.heading: .—Provider
children.label: 3 .—Provider
children.text: The term “provider” means a person that provides domestic launch services or domestic reentry services to the Government.

children.heading: .—Reentry services
children.label: 4 .—Reentry services
children.text: The term “reentry services” has the meaning given the term in section 50902.

children.heading: .—Related entity
children.label: 5 .—Related entity
children.text: The term “related entity” means a contractor or subcontractor.

children.label: A
children.text: the United States Government;

children.label: B
children.text: related entities of the Government involved in launch services or reentry services;

children.label: C
children.text: a provider;

children.label: D
children.text: related entities of the provider involved in launch services or reentry services; or

children.label: E
children.text: a government astronaut.
